name: Rclone
categoryDescription: Commonly abused tools that leave NO Windows registry artifacts. These tools are typically single executables, portable builds, or in-memory frameworks. Detection depends entirely on non-registry evidence: process execution logs, prefetch, ShimCache/Amcache, BAM/DAM, network traffic, and memory forensics. Includes detection notes for each tool.
description: Single executable cloud sync tool supporting 70+ storage providers. Frequently renamed by threat actors (e.g., svchost.exe, chrome.exe) to blend in. No installer, no registry entries.
forensic_value: No registry artifacts. Common command patterns: rclone copy C:\staging remote:bucket; rclone sync. Renaming is standard — hunt by PE hash or behavioral signatures rather than filename.
